results.title: Planet Carrier: Anatomy, Load Paths, and Design Trade-Offs
results.snippet: Planet Carrier Anatomy A typical carrier contains five to seven distinct precision features: arms, plates, planet pin bores, bearing journals, a central hub bore, lubrication channels, and retention features. Arms, Plates, and Windows The carrier body extends multiple arms outward from a central hub

results.title: IMS Gear: Customizable Planetary Gears for Every Industry Need
results.snippet: A planetary gear (also: epicyclic gear) is a technical design that serves as a "link" for power transmission between a drive source (the motor) and an application to be moved (e.g. the movement of a wheel).

results.title: Planetary Gear: Design, Parts, Function, Ratio & Uses [PDF]

results.title: Space-saving planetary gearboxes for high torques - Rollstar
results.snippet: How a planetary gear works In its simplest form, a planetary gear consists of three coaxial shafts that interact with each other via specially arranged gears. Planetary gears are epicyclic gear systems whose shafts and axles revolve on circular paths in the frame, the so-called planetary carrier.
